free_text: Dipole strength distribution of 74Ge
ELBE
Ee=7.0 MeV (390 uA) on Nb foil (7 um thick), or
Ee=12.1 MeV (440 uA) on Nb foil (12.5 um thick).
Low-energy part of the bremsstrahlung spectrum was
reduced by Al absorber (10 cm thick) behind the Nb
radiator.
964.9 mg enriched 74Ge disk (2 cm in diam.)
199.0 mg enriched 11B disk (2 cm in diam.) for
flux determination
Efficiency of ~100% relative to a NaI detector
(7.6 cm diam., 7.6 cm long).
Two HPGe detectors at 90 deg relative to the photon
beam direction and at distance of 28 cm from the
target.
Two HPGe detectors at 127 deg relative to the photon
beam direction and at distance of 32 cm from the
target.
Absorbers (8 mm Pb+3 mm Cu at 90 deg, 3 mm Pb+3 mm Cu
at 127 deg) were placed in front of HPGe detectors
to reduce the count rate due to low-energy photons.
All HPGe detectors were surrounded by BGO
scintillation detectors (3 cm thick).
Systematic uncertainties (e.g., efficiency
and flux) and statistics.
Approved by Ralph Massarczyk (2016-02-08)
On. Data received from Ralph Massarczyk.
On
Measured at 127 deg and corrected for Doppler-shift
90 deg/127 deg intensity ratio
Measured at 127 deg.Ee=7.0 and 12.1 MeV for levels
below and above 6.0 MeV
The following transitions are treated as inelastic
transitions and excluded:
- The sum of the energies of the transition and the
first or second 2+ state fits the energy of a
higher-lying transition within the uncertainties.
- The transition with energy up to 6.0 MeV observed in
the 12.1 MeV measurement but not in the 7.0 MeV
measurement.
Parity assignment uncertain
Spin assignment uncertain
Spin assigned by A.Jung et al.(1995)
Spin of 7148.7 keV level and parity of all levels
Table I of Phys.Rev.C92(2015)044309
Obtained by Ee=7.0 MeV irradiation
Obtained by Ee=12.1 MeV irradiation
Absorption (scattering) cross section was derived by
dividing the (g,g0) elastic scattering cross section
by the ground state transition branching ratio.
Selection of ground state transitions and estimation
of ground state transition branching ratios were done
by simulating gamma-ray cascades by the Monte Carlo
code DICEBOX with the level schemes obtained by the
code gammaDEX.
Plotted in Fig.6 of Phys.Rev.C92(2015)044309
